What entities are present on the slide titled 'Design Purpose' and what details do they show?
canonical_answer: A presentation slide displays the title 'Design Purpose' with four numbered points listing ergonomic issues. Images show wrists bending upwards and inward, marked with red lines and checkmarks. A keyboard layout diagram highlights specific keys with red circles. Three line drawings at the bottom illustrate wrist rotation positions labeled Supination, Handshake position, and Pronation.

question: What visual elements and text are included on the slide explaining 'MID (Molded Interconnect Device)'?
canonical_answer: A presentation slide displays the title 'MID (Molded Interconnect Device)' with a circular diagram of five numbered steps. An illustration depicts a hand gripping a mug with a red triangular section highlighted. Text on the right lists advantages such as miniaturization and weight reduction. Diagrams at the bottom show segmented structures labeled 'Curved' and 'Flat'.

question: Describe the sequence of slides shown from the start of the presentation until the design purpose details are visible.
canonical_answer: At 0s, a white slide with a university logo and bold Korean title text appears; at 2s, the slide remains static with student IDs listed below; at 12s, a table of contents slide with five chapters is shown; at 14s, a design purpose slide featuring wrist diagrams and red accents appears; at 16s, specific ergonomic problem points are highlighted with images.

question: Describe the specific diagrams and text points that appear on the design purpose slide during the presentation.
canonical_answer: At 12s, a slide listing five chapters in Korean and English is displayed; at 14s, text explaining Carpal Tunnel Syndrome causes appears on a white background; at 16s, images showing wrists bending upwards and inward are presented; at 18s, diagrams labeled Supination, Handshake position, and Pronation are visible at the bottom right.
